title: Day 2 · Real model + tool_use / tool_result
subtitle: A compact map of Day 2's core changes: a real Provider, Anthropic content blocks, and tool_result returning as the next user message.

diagrams.label: Main loop
diagrams.title: The real-model tool-calling loop
diagrams.summary: The prompt enters messages, AnthropicProvider returns tool_use, ToolRegistry runs system_date, tool_result returns to messages, and the model produces final text.

nodes.label: CLI Runtime
nodes.detail: CLI Runtime still only catches user input. Day 2 changes the provider and the messages protocol shape, not the CLI boundary.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: messages is the context sent to the Messages API. The first round has only the user question; after tool execution, it also contains assistant tool_use and user tool_result.

nodes.label: AnthropicProvider
nodes.detail: AnthropicProvider is only a protocol adapter: it sends messages and tool schemas to an Anthropic-compatible API, then translates content blocks into the internal ModelResponse. It does not execute tools.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: The model sees the system_date input_schema, knows it has no wall clock, and returns a tool_use content block. This is a proposal, not execution.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.detail: The harness-side ToolRegistry is what actually executes system_date. Day 2 still only does registry lookup; permission checks arrive in Day 5.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: ToolResult stores the system_date return value. The next step is not role='tool'; it is a tool_result content block inside the next user message.

nodes.label: stdout
nodes.detail: After the second complete(messages), the model reads the tool_result and returns final text. The CLI prints final and the Agent Loop exits.

diagrams.label: Message shape
diagrams.title: Anthropic tool_use / tool_result message shape
diagrams.summary: The key Day 2 protocol change: tool_result is no longer Day 1's role='tool'; it is a content block inside the next role='user' message.

nodes.label: user prompt
nodes.detail: The user still writes natural language. The CLI does not pre-decide system_date; the real model asks for the tool after seeing the tools schema.

nodes.label: messages[0]
nodes.detail: The first messages entry is still a normal user message. AnthropicProvider passes it through to the Messages API.

nodes.label: assistant message
nodes.detail: The model returns an assistant content block with type='tool_use', id, name, and input. The harness must append this whole assistant message back into messages.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry.run
nodes.detail: The harness reads name/input from the assistant tool_use and routes it to ToolRegistry.run(call). The model still has not executed any Python function.

nodes.label: user message
nodes.detail: This is the Day 2 protocol hinge: tool_result lives inside the next role='user' content block and points back to the previous tool_use id. It is not Day 1's role='tool'.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: On the second Messages API call, the model reads the tool_result content block and turns the observation into final text.

nodes.label: stdout
nodes.detail: The later Terminal Replay shows the terminal-facing tool_call, observation, and final output. This diagram only explains the protocol shape inside messages.

diagrams.label: Provider boundary
diagrams.title: AnthropicProvider as the protocol adapter
diagrams.summary: External Anthropic/DeepSeek content blocks stop inside AnthropicProvider; the Agent Loop continues to work with internal ModelResponse / ToolCall / ToolResult.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: run_agent hands accumulated messages to the provider. The provider can change, but the Agent Loop input boundary stays stable.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry.list
nodes.detail: ToolRegistry.list() exposes available tools and parameters. Day 2's new parameters field becomes Anthropic input_schema.

nodes.label: AnthropicProvider
nodes.detail: AnthropicProvider translates Tool(parameters) into Anthropic tools/input_schema and sends messages through messages.create.

nodes.label: Messages API
nodes.detail: The request can go to DeepSeek's Anthropic-compatible endpoint or the official Claude API. The harness only relies on the Anthropic Messages API shape.

nodes.label: content blocks
nodes.detail: The external API returns text blocks and tool_use blocks; DeepSeek may also return thinking blocks. The provider keeps assistant_content intact for the next request.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: This is the provider boundary's output: external content blocks are translated into the internal ModelResponse. run_agent does not need to know the vendor response object.

nodes.label: run_agent
nodes.detail: run_agent only depends on the ModelProvider Protocol and the internal ModelResponse. Day 2 swaps in a real model without rewriting the Agent Loop shape.
